ai_description: Ohtani shatters records with a 60-60 season.
summary:
Shohei Ohtani has made history by becoming the first player in MLB history to record 60 home runs and 60 stolen bases in a single season. This achievement cements his legacy and showcases his exceptional skills, with some arguing it surpasses the accomplishments of other legendary athletes like Jordan and Brady. Ohtani's impressive performance is a testament to his elite level of play, both on the mound and at the plate.

* Shohei Ohtani achieved a historic 60-60 season, recording 60 home runs and 60 stolen bases in a single season.
* Ohtani's accomplishment is considered one of the greatest in sports history, rivaling the achievements of legendary athletes like Michael Jordan and Tom Brady.
* This milestone demonstrates Ohtani's exceptional abilities as both a hitter and a pitcher, solidifying his position as one of the most talented players in the game.
